subtitle: Arupadai Veedu
summary: Hill temple dedicated to Dhandayuthapani Swamy; one of the six sacred abodes.
history: Pazhamudircholai? no, Palani is the third abode of Murugan associated with renunciation and wisdom.
spiritualMeaning: Hill temple dedicated to Dhandayuthapani Swamy; one of the six sacred abodes.
practices: Prefer early morning darshan on festival days

subtitle: Senthil Andavar
summary: Tiruchendur is the sacred seashore abode of Lord Murugan, associated with victory over Surapadman and the triumph of dharma.
history: The temple is traditionally linked with the divine battle against Surapadman. Its ocean-side setting makes it unique among the six abodes and symbolizes courage, purification and divine protection.
spiritualMeaning: Tiruchendur represents the power to overcome inner negativity, fear and confusion through faith and righteous action.

subtitle: Swaminatha Swamy
summary: Swamimalai celebrates Lord Murugan as the divine teacher who explains the meaning of Pranava to Lord Shiva.
history: The tradition of Swamimalai presents Murugan as Guru, showing that wisdom is not limited by age or form. The temple inspires learning, humility and respect for knowledge.
spiritualMeaning: Swamimalai teaches that spiritual learning requires humility. Even the highest truth must be received with openness.

subtitle: Subramanya Swamy
summary: Thiruparankundram is traditionally associated with the divine wedding of Lord Murugan and Deivanai.
history: This ancient rock-cut shrine near Madurai is celebrated as a sacred place of divine union, duty and auspicious beginnings.
spiritualMeaning: The temple reminds devotees that devotion must mature into responsibility, harmony and service.

subtitle: Thanigai Murugan
summary: Thiruthani represents peace after victory and the cooling of anger after battle.
history: The hill temple is connected with Lord Murugan's calm and gracious form after the divine battle. It is a place devotees approach for mental peace and strength.
spiritualMeaning: Thiruthani teaches that true victory includes inner peace, emotional control and forgiveness.

subtitle: Solai Malai Murugan
summary: Pazhamudircholai is a green hill shrine celebrating Murugan with Valli and Deivanai.
history: Surrounded by natural beauty, this shrine represents abundance, devotion and the unity of divine love and wisdom.
spiritualMeaning: Pazhamudircholai reminds devotees that spiritual life can be joyful, balanced and rooted in nature.

subtitle: Arupadai Veedu
summary: One of the six abodes, uniquely located beside the sea.
history: Seashore temple where Murugan is worshipped after victory over Surapadman.
spiritualMeaning: One of the six abodes, uniquely located beside the sea.
practices: Prefer early morning darshan on festival days
Respect temple dress and photography rules
